# SafeML

> SafeML is a research project for runtime safety monitoring of machine learning classifiers using empirical distribution distance measures.

Canonical documentation: https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
Source repository: https://github.com/ISorokos/SafeML
License: MIT

## Documentation

- [Overview](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting-started/)
- [Installation](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting-started/installation/)
- [Quick Start](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting-started/quick-start/)
- [Usage Examples](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/guides/)
- [API Reference](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/api/)
- [FAQ](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/faq/)
- [Citation](https://safeml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/project/citation/)

SafeML compares statistical characteristics from trusted training data with characteristics computed from incoming operational data. The repository includes Python, MATLAB, and R implementations. It is experimental research software and does not by itself certify a system as safe.
